subject: Civics (Social and Political Life - II)
chapter: Growing Up as Boys and Girls

activities_projects.title: Cross-Cultural Childhood Comparison
activities_projects.objective: Compare gender roles in Samoa and 1960s Madhya Pradesh
steps: Read the case studies of Samoa (1920s) and Madhya Pradesh (1960s) from the text
Compare them based on: 'Who looked after children?', 'Where did boys and girls play?', and 'When did they start outdoor work?'
Analyze why Samoan girls had more freedom to go on fishing trips compared to MP girls
Conclude how 'growing up' is not the same everywhere
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Analyzes how social environments and time periods shape gender identities
activities_projects.evaluation: Detail and accuracy in the comparison table (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Domestic Labor Time-Log
activities_projects.objective: Quantify the time spent on unpaid domestic work
steps: Interview a male and a female adult in the family about their daily schedule
Record the hours spent on: a) Paid work (Office/Shop) and b) Unpaid work (Cleaning/Cooking/Care)
Calculate the total 'working hours' for both, including housework
Discuss if the 'unpaid work' is seen as less valuable by the family
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes the concept of 'invisible labor' and the disparity in leisure time
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the time-log and quality of the conclusion (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Deconstructing Toy Advertisements
activities_projects.objective: Identify how media reinforces gender stereotypes
steps: Find ads for 'Action Figures/Cars' and 'Dolls/Kitchen Sets'
Note the colors used (Blue vs. Pink) and the verbs used in the text (Build/Win vs. Care/Share)
Write a paragraph on how these ads prepare boys and girls for 'different roles' in adulthood
Design a 'Gender-Neutral' toy advertisement
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Critically examines media influence on gendered expectations
activities_projects.evaluation: Analysis of ad elements and creativity in the neutral design (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The 'Double Burden' Case Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Understand the challenges of working women
materials: Story of Melani (domestic worker) from the textbook
steps: Read about Melani’s life and her dual responsibilities
Identify what is meant by 'Double Burden' based on her story
Research why many domestic workers are women and why their wages are often low
Suggest three ways society can help reduce the double burden for women
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Explains why domestic and care-giving work is often devalued in the economy
activities_projects.evaluation: Definition of double burden and quality of suggestions (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Classroom Space & Safety Mapping
activities_projects.objective: Evaluate the use of public space by different genders
steps: Observe the playground: Which areas are used by boys for sports?
Identify where girls stand or play during the break
Interview girls to find out if they feel 'safe' or 'watched' in open streets/playgrounds
Relate this to the MP school case study where girls used streets only to reach home
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands how social norms dictate the use of public and private spaces
activities_projects.evaluation: Observation skills and ability to relate findings to the text (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Valuing Housework: The Economic Argument
activities_projects.objective: Translate domestic work into economic terms
materials: Local labor rates (e.g., cost of a cook, cleaner, or nanny per hour)
steps: List all tasks done by a 'housewife' in a day
Assign a market value to these tasks using local rates (e.g., Cooking = ₹100/meal)
Calculate the 'Monthly Salary' a mother/housewife would earn if she were paid
Discuss why society assumes this work comes 'naturally' to women and doesn't need pay
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Challenges the idea that housework is not 'real work' because it is unpaid
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical calculation and depth of the discussion on 'natural' roles (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Government Policy Audit: Crèches and Anganwadis
activities_projects.objective: Identify legal steps toward gender equality
steps: Research the law that mandates a 'Crèche' (childcare) for companies with more than 30 women
Find out the location of the nearest Anganwadi in your area
Explain how childcare facilities help women achieve equality in the workplace
Create a poster showing 'Rights for Working Mothers'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes that equality requires government intervention and legal support
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct facts about crèche laws and quality of the poster (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Gendered Language Investigation
steps: Listen to common phrases like 'Don't cry like a girl' or 'Boys will be boys'
Analyze what these phrases imply about the emotions of boys and girls
Find three examples of gender-neutral language (e.g., 'Firefighter' instead of 'Fireman')
Write a short dialogue between a parent and child that avoids gender stereotypes
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies how language reinforces and perpetuates gender roles
activities_projects.evaluation: Ability to identify bias and suggest inclusive alternatives (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Care-giving Sector Survey
activities_projects.objective: Link gender roles to specific career choices
steps: Find out the percentage of women vs. men in nursing or primary teaching
Find out the percentage of women vs. men in heavy engineering or piloting
Explain the link between 'home-care' roles and the jobs women are 'expected' to do
Discuss if these 'care' jobs are paid as well as 'technical' jobs
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects household gender roles to horizontal segregation in the labor market
activities_projects.evaluation: Analysis of data and the 'care vs. technical' wage gap (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Debate: 'Equality begins at Home'
steps: Form two teams: one for 'Traditional Roles' and one for 'Shared Responsibility'
Arguments must cover: Work-life balance, children’s upbringing, and fairness
Include the point from the text that 'equality is a central principle of our Constitution'
Propose a 'Family Chore Chart' where everyone participates regardless of gender
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops a constitutional perspective on equality within the private sphere
activities_projects.evaluation: Strength of arguments and use of constitutional principles (10 marks)
